title: OpenTelemetry in Depth
subtitle: Architecting Modern Observability
author: Wordsus Team
description: A deep dive into OpenTelemetry, the industry standard for observability. Learn how to architect, implement, and scale telemetry pipelines using traces, metrics, and logs to gain deep insights into distributed systems.

chapters.title: Introduction to Modern Observability
chapters.description: Explore the shift from traditional monitoring to modern observability and the critical role of high-fidelity telemetry.

chapters.title: The OpenTelemetry Project and Ecosystem
chapters.description: Understand the history, governance, and components of the CNCF OpenTelemetry project.

chapters.title: Core Architecture and Concepts
chapters.description: Master the fundamental building blocks of OTel, including APIs, SDKs, OTLP, and context propagation.

chapters.title: Distributed Tracing Fundamentals
chapters.description: Deep dive into traces, spans, and parent-child relationships for tracking requests across microservices.

chapters.title: Metrics and Time-Series Data
chapters.description: Learn to collect and aggregate time-series data using various metric instruments and temporality models.

chapters.title: Logging in the OpenTelemetry Era
chapters.description: Integrate traditional logging into the OTel ecosystem with standardized data models and bridges.

chapters.title: Signal Correlation and Context
chapters.description: Harness the power of unified telemetry by linking traces, logs, and metrics for root cause analysis.

chapters.title: Automatic Instrumentation
chapters.description: Leverage zero-code solutions and bytecode manipulation to collect telemetry without modifying application code.

chapters.title: Manual Instrumentation APIs and SDKs
chapters.description: Take full control of your telemetry by implementing custom instrumentation using OTel APIs.

chapters.title: Instrumenting Web and Mobile Applications
chapters.description: Extend observability to the frontend with RUM, browser instrumentation, and mobile SDKs.

chapters.title: Instrumenting Storage and External Systems
chapters.description: Standardize telemetry collection for databases, caches, and messaging systems.

chapters.title: Collector Architecture and Deployment Models
chapters.description: Architect resilient telemetry pipelines using agents, gateways, and custom collector builds.

chapters.title: Receivers: Ingesting Telemetry
chapters.description: Configure receivers to ingest data from OTLP, Prometheus, and other legacy protocols.

chapters.title: Processors: Mutating and Filtering Data
chapters.description: Transform, redact, and batch your data in-flight using OTTL and resource processors.

chapters.title: Exporters and Routing
chapters.description: Route telemetry to multiple backends efficiently using gRPC, HTTP, and vendor-specific exporters.

chapters.title: Advanced Sampling Strategies
chapters.description: Optimize costs and performance with head-based, tail-based, and adaptive sampling techniques.

chapters.title: Securing the Telemetry Pipeline
chapters.description: Implement TLS, authentication, and RBAC to protect sensitive observability data.

chapters.title: Performance Tuning and Scalability
chapters.description: Size and monitor your collector clusters to handle high-throughput production workloads.

chapters.title: OpenTelemetry in Kubernetes
chapters.description: Deploy the OTel Operator and automate instrumentation within Kubernetes environments.

chapters.title: Cloud Provider Integrations
chapters.description: Leverage managed OTel distributions from AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure.

chapters.title: Connecting to Observability Backends
chapters.description: Integrate with Jaeger, Prometheus, Grafana, and popular commercial SaaS platforms.

chapters.title: Developing Custom Collector Components
chapters.description: Extend the collector's capabilities by writing custom receivers, processors, and exporters in Go.

chapters.title: Emerging Signals and Beyond
chapters.description: Explore the future of observability, including continuous profiling and eBPF-based insights.

parts.title: Part I: Foundations of Observability and OpenTelemetry
Part V: Advanced Configuration and Optimization
Part VII: The Future and Extending OpenTelemetry
